title: ft_atoi
subject:
Write a program that takes one string and prints the integer it represents, followed by a newline, the way the standard atoi() reads it: skip leading whitespace (spaces and tabs), then an optional '+' or '-' sign, then digits, stopping at the first non-digit.

If the number of arguments is not exactly 1, print just a newline.

Examples:
  $ ./a.out "42"
  42
  $ ./a.out "   -123abc"
  -123
  $ ./a.out "+7"
  7
